title: Optimized safeTransferFrom with non-standard ERC20 support and EOA checks
solidity_before:
(bool success, bytes memory data) = token.call(abi.encodeWithSelector(IERC20.transferFrom.selector, from, to, amount));
require(success && (data.length == 0 || abi.decode(data, (bool))), "TransferFromFailed");
explanation: Constructs the `transferFrom` calldata tightly in memory. Executes the call and handles non-standard ERC20s (like USDT, which returns no data) by checking if `returndatasize()` is 0. Critically, it also checks `extcodesize` to prevent silent successes if the `token` address is an EOA (Externally Owned Account) rather than a contract.
when_to_apply: Any time a contract needs to pull tokens from a user while supporting weird ERC20 tokens.
when_not_to_apply: If the protocol strictly whitelists only standard-compliant ERC20 tokens.
